response.justification: James Smith is a capable full-stack software developer with broad technical range, but falls significantly short of the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) requirements. The role demands a Director-level individual contributor with deep, recognized AI expertise and a track record of strategic advisory and thought leadership — none of which are evident in the resume. His AI experience is limited to his current role at re:cinq (started April 2025), and while MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling are relevant signals, they do not constitute the 'demonstrated products or platforms built with AI' the JD specifies. He holds two Bachelor's degrees with no graduate-level AI education, and his career arc reflects strong individual execution at the Senior/Staff level rather than the strategic influence, external advisory capacity, and industry recognition this role requires.
strengths: Recent hands-on AI agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflows at re:cinq) shows genuine current engagement with the AI tooling space
Long entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective and contributor to multiple open-source projects demonstrates a builder and initiative-driven mindset
Active community engagement through public conference talks (FOSS4G) and multi-day workshops indicates some external knowledge-sharing and communication capability
concerns: AI expertise is extremely shallow — limited to approximately one year at re:cinq with no prior history of building or shipping AI-powered products or platforms at scale
Career history is a Senior/Staff IC developer profile with no evidence of strategic advisory roles, customer-facing executive engagement, or Director-level influence
No graduate degree in an AI-related field and no demonstrated industry recognition (publications, keynotes, analyst coverage) as an AI expert or thought leader

key_factors.factor: Insufficient AI expertise depth and track record
Recent AI agent and MCP tooling work
